On top of helping to hit this goal, here’s what your donation would help accomplish:
* Growing our grassroots campaign so we can keep up our outreach and support Democrats up and down the ticket
* Building momentum for big, bold action — like universal child care, a wealth tax, a guaranteed nationwide right to an abortion, ethics reform at the Supreme Court, and a whole lot more
* Taking power out of the hands of special interests, greedy CEOs, and wealthy insiders and putting it where it belongs: in the hands of working people

Yeah, there’s a lot of trouble in our nation. There are threats to our safety, to our economic security, to our health, and to our very democracy. And when there’s this much trouble, we can whimper. We can whine. Or we can fight back. Me? I’m fighting back.
The first step in fighting back is defeating Republicans and electing Democrats up and down the ticket. And our next big test is tonight’s monthly grassroots fundraising deadline.
[_**Every donation puts this team in a better position to win. So, if it makes sense for you right now, will you pitch in $3 or whatever you can to support our re-election campaign and boost Democrats across the country?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62580599/403924578/1737578033?amount=3&refcode=EMZ13K_EOM_E_20230430_V1S1_F&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDgvMS84NzkzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlN2Q2MmZlMC1iMGU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PKUBBE3bG181KfHi3orWNcut5a7VK5W9fEd1e26HyWI=)

Elizabeth

clean_body: To stay on track and grow our re-election campaign, it’s powerfully important that we reach every target | | | I'll get right to it: = | If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: | | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = On top of helping to hit this goal, here’s what your donation would help accomplish: Growing our grassroots campaign so we can keep up our outreach and support Democrats up and down the ticket Building momentum for big, bold action — like universal child care, a wealth tax, a guaranteed nationwide right to an abortion, ethics reform at the Supreme Court, and a whole lot more * Taking power out of the hands of special interests, greedy CEOs, and wealthy insiders and putting it where it belongs: in the hands of working people Yeah, there’s a lot of trouble in our nation. There are threats to our safety, to our economic security, to our health, and to our very democracy. And when there’s this much trouble, we can whimper. We can whine. Or we can fight back. Me? I’m fighting back. The first step in fighting back is defeating Republicans and electing Democrats up and down the ticket. And our next big test is tonight’s monthly grassroots fundraising deadline. = Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth = | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for Senate All content © 2023 Warren for Senate, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

name: Lateefah Simon
subject: This is the key to reaching our goals
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LATEEFAH FOR CONGRESS
body:
Tonight at midnight is our end-of-month fundraising deadline.
# [](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/go/39?t=1&akid=21%2E1163%2EnuVeYI)
Since it's the weekend, I'll make this quick:
Tonight at midnight is our end-of-month fundraising deadline. I'm going to organize in every single community in California's 12th district. My goal is to reach every single voter and spread our message of continued progress -- and, to be successful, we need a robust budget to prepare for anything that comes our way. **Engaging our online community is _the_ key to reaching our fundraising goals.**
I'm so grateful to know that you, like so many of my friends and neighbors, are part of our team. **[If you're able to today, will you chip in any amount to power our movement to win representation in Washington?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230430-1&refcode=em20230430-1?refcode2=21_1163_nuVeYI&akid=21%2E1163%2EnuVeYI)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230430-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230430-1&refcode2=21_1163_nuVeYI&akid=21%2E1163%2EnuVeYI)**
Thank you for everything you do. If you're not able to chip in right now, we're glad you read updates from our team. But if you are in a position to give, **[I'd be extremely grateful.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230430-1&refcode=em20230430-1?refcode2=21_1163_nuVeYI&akid=21%2E1163%2EnuVeYI)**
Together,
Lateefah Simon
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em-wrapper&refcode=em20230430-1?t=2&refcode2=21_1163_nuVeYI&akid=21%2E1163%2EnuVeYI)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=21%2E1163%2EnuVeYI)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=21%2E1163%2EnuVeYI)

To give by check:
Lateefah for Congress
1714 Franklin St #100 - 438,
Oakland, CA, 94612
Email us: [info@lateefahsimon.com](mailto:info@lateefahsimon.com)

My goal is to reach every single voter and spread our message of continued progress -- and, to be successful, we need a robust budget to prepare for anything that comes our way. Engaging our online community is the key to reaching our fundraising goals. I'm so grateful to know that you, like so many of my friends and neighbors, are part of our team. Thank you for everything you do. If you're not able to chip in right now, we're glad you read updates from our team. But if you are in a position to give, Together, Lateefah Simon

name: Sharice Davids
subject: Cutting right to the chase
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
| I’ll cut right to it: We have just hours to hit our April budget goal and still need to raise $3,000. **Last cycle, millions of dollars were spent to try and flip our district red, but our grassroots organizing was strong, so we were able to etch out an impressive victory.**

Now, we’ve already been named a top GOP target for 2024, which means our race is going to be just as critical as 2022. And starting our work early is essential.

**So will you please rush $20 or more now to help us hit our important goal and take back the House?

Here's the best link to donate:**

**[https://act.shariceforcongress.com/a/april23-goal](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62574195/403902063/1004362382?amount=20&contactdata=RrhcyHfdk6ikizrCovQtl78BMMHUvumQnKTtD7PcylxOrbf67mIc+nSQCG9W9yN1B+T7VM6wbbjVdp0jyYuf0KIjvBW/QyP3+UcSiPOeo1JXpAuoq9JwMCpcLVCBwG2s32a5WqG7Tl2rvuqxul1CJw==&source=EM_NBI_SD_20230430_FR.eom.2.8_0_6MO_candidate_NA_NA_eom&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MTdmMjg5ZS05MWU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3VNacnIOkFl2how5HIxf5RdDMoBPkv20GriMCPjuPZU=)**

— Sharice

name: =?utf-8?Q?Gabe=20Amo?=
subject: My High School Football Team
body:
Help Us Reach Our Monthly Goal by Midnight | | | |
---
| | | Hi Friend,

When I announced my candidacy for Congress, I was the 14th person to enter the race.

Fun fact: 14 was also my number in high school. I wasn’t very good at football, but we won a state championship because we were all in it together — as a team.

In our campaign for Congress, we’re also building an amazing team — including you! And I’m asking for your help as today marks our April fundraising goal deadline. [](https://gabeamo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cc42fee5a4ff30fb7e2aead18&id=80b70e1635&e=9651074c98) **I’m in the race because I know that together we can make huge progress on the issues that matter most — including affordable health care, housing, reproductive freedom, and safety from gun violence.**

Please consider [chipping in today by midnight](https://gabeamo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cc42fee5a4ff30fb7e2aead18&id=cafdf0253c&e=9651074c98) for the strongest fundraising month possible. **[](https://gabeamo.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cc42fee5a4ff30fb7e2aead18&id=d41e646b98&e=9651074c98)** In gratitude,

Gabe Amo

body:
Hey team, our end-of-month deadline is almost here, but we're not quite where we need to be.

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/Kmd5WHhkg3_fZv3Xt7w6mb4SCpdZI6Fek0FcKU_QMgk=/k6RLjJH9xByc7J462yWiXJR8utpZCg4Gn3sGsZY_M84=?refcode=EM_FR_230430EOM5_DK_90DO_2_NG_N_Logo)
Hi, fellow Democrats -- we've emailed you a few times now about our end-of-month fundraising deadline, and we've almost met our goal. We are so thankful for your support!

I know you've been generous, but for the good of the Party, I need to ask again. **Our ActBlue shows we are still about $749 short of where we need to be.**

**[Please, can you chip in $10 or more ASAP to help us close that gap and invest in Illinois Democrats? We're fighting for the future of Illinois -- and ordinary people like you are fueling this movement every step of the way.](https://act.myngp.com/el/Kmd5WHhkg3_fZv3Xt7w6mb4SCpdZI6Fek0FcKU_QMgk=/k6RLjJH9xByc7J462yWiXJR8utpZCg4Gn3sGsZY_M84=?refcode=EM_FR_230430EOM5_DK_90DO_2_NG_N_L1&amount=10)**

**GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

body:

name: from Rep. Dana Criswell
subject: Corporate Welfare - For it or against it?
body:
Corporate Welfare - is any financial benefit purposely granted by the government to a specific business or class of businesses and that is not generally available to all businesses and taxpayers. I've never heard a political candidate campaign on the positives of crony-capitalism or corporate welfare. Both democrats and republicans seem to agree in theory that corporate welfare is bad. A quick search of the internet will find statements made by

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/72ae5dce-6880-43b4-a4da-cd61699be921?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Corporate Welfare -[any financial benefit](https://substack.com/redirect/d2e62c94-8bcc-46a8-94b2-134e71800a32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) purposely granted by the government to a specific business or class of businesses and that is not generally available to all businesses and taxpayers._
I've never heard a political candidate campaign on support of crony-capitalism or corporate welfare. Both democrats and republicans seem to agree, in theory, that corporate welfare is bad. A quick search of the internet will find statements made by [the CATO Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/b087a7b8-6761-4f9d-a7dd-d6319b1c048a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a conservative think-tank, calling for the end of corporate welfare. In that same Google search you will also find extreme-liberal Robert B. Reich writing about how "[Corporate Welfare Hurts You](https://substack.com/redirect/668288b4-b4bc-4cf5-8430-d96abdfa9e21?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)". So if we all agree that corporate welfare is bad, why do politicians continue to take millions from you to transfer to their corporate buddies?
Thanks for reading Dana Criswell! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribe](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGFuYWNyaXN3ZWxsLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTE3Mzg4Njc2LCJzIjoxNTI2NzgxLCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNjgyODg3MjU3LCJleHAiOjE2ODU0NzkyNTcsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.5QUSMUuFbuPniI6tBl1TwFZnfMhn739AncGSe49RLWw?)
I doubt many are aware that corporate welfare was invented in Mississippi. Maybe that is why Mississippi seems so good at giving away our money.
In 1936 Governor Hugh White expanded a local program he had devised as mayor of Columbia. His [Balance Agriculture with Industry (BAWI)](https://substack.com/redirect/7e6c7cec-4330-4284-bb91-1f5a5d264db4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) plan was the first in the nation to use state funds to recruit industry. To overcome the hurdle of the Mississippi Constitution explicitly prohibiting the state from using credit to finance industrial development, Gov. White organized a team of politicians and attorneys to devise his plan.
The BAWI plan only lasted three years before it was abandoned. It became evident that the 12 industries and approximately 2700 jobs it recruited did not accomplish what many expected. Those in state government concluded that jobs were not actually created but simply moved from one industry to another. They also concluded that the money spent by the state to recruit was not offset by any benefit to the state.
Years later despite overwhelming evidence that these programs cost taxpayers much more than they produce, other states followed Mississippi's lead down the path of state-funded incentives to draw businesses to their state. These politicians proved once again that they never let negative results for citizens stand in their way.
Now we find states in a race to provide ever increasing incentives, tax relief and even direct payments to businesses who have the connections needed to lobby the politicians for their special deals.
Mississippi has now re-entered the corporate welfare race. The [Mississippi Development Authority](https://substack.com/redirect/cbc1148a-90d2-43fd-8c44-c0cd3761b772?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (MDA) is the lead state government agency that "works to recruit new business to the state and retain and expand existing Mississippi industry and business." The MDA has provided billions of taxpayer-funded tax breaks, grants, and loans to hundreds of businesses. Proponents point to jobs created but neglect to consider the total cost to the state. In addition, the idea of the state choosing winners and losers in business in contrary to the basic free market principles held by Republicans.
The [Mississippi GOP ](https://substack.com/redirect/6f647366-2d34-4efb-89fd-fc4dbeb088f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)platform states;
_We believe that free enterprise will thrive, flourish and benefit more people through a framework of low taxes and sensible but not overburdening regulations, which both encourages the expansion of existing businesses while also attracts new companies to locate in Mississippi. _
That does not sound like a policy of tax breaks, tax incentives, and special treatment for chosen businesses.
[John Stossel](https://substack.com/redirect/962d4e93-a7ea-4129-b49b-96b21dade4a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reported when Memphis, TN gave IKEA $9.5 million in tax breaks because of a promise to hire 175 people, locally owned furniture store owners protested. "What about us?" asks Ron Becker, owner of The Great American Home Store. "We pay taxes here. Where is our financial incentive?"
So why do politicians campaign against corporate welfare but continue to subsidize it once elected? The answer is simple. It gives the local and state politicians opportunities to gain favor with large industry leaders, which relates to campaign donations, and it gives them the opportunity to claim credit for new jobs. Politicians love ribbon-cutting ceremonies, they provide them with occasions where the press puts their picture on the front page of the local newspaper.
Mississippi has remained in last place economically for decades and will not move forward until it's citizens demand we do something different. Old, out-dated, unsuccessful programs like the MDA and its predecessor the BAWI have proven to be harmful to the citizens of Mississippi.
Its time to make a change.
Thanks for reading Dana Criswell! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribe](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGFuYWNyaXN3ZWxsLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTE3Mzg4Njc2LCJzIjoxNTI2NzgxLCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNjgyODg3MjU3LCJleHAiOjE2ODU0NzkyNTcsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.5QUSMUuFbuPniI6tBl1TwFZnfMhn739AncGSe49RLWw?)

| | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62575714/403908128/-2112663142?refcode=EM_BS_LEE_230430_FR_BattleBorn_A0_NA_U1_X1_M1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MjA5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NmZmZjJlNC05N2U3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Juo6f-uFi__vynnRGp1JkfexncYQeJhPhXlZMgaMSGo=) Folks, Nevada first achieved statehood during the height of the Civil War. Ever since, we’ve been known as the “Battle Born State.” It’s a fitting title. Nevadans can tackle just about any challenge. Heck, at the height of summer, sometimes just going outside to walk the dog is a test of our mettle! I’ve lived in Nevada for going on thirty years, and I’m no stranger to a battle myself – I represent a battleground district in Congress. Last year, NV-03 was one of the most competitive districts in the country, and I defeated my GOP opponent in November by _just 3.96%!_ That narrow margin of victory is exactly why Republicans are already filling their war chest to the brim with MAGA millions. | Have you seen this, team?

We are gearing up for a competitive year in 2024, and dark money-backed Republicans are already outraising Democrats. To fight back, we set an ambitious $14,000 end-of-month fundraising goal. We're looking for a final surge of grassroots donations just to push us over the edge.

**[Will you chip in $5 before tonight's deadline? Every little bit moves us closer to our goal!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62575802/403908295/-1720848229?refcode=em23_230430_eom3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230430_eom3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=05a2559a-98e7-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&amounts=5,10,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNWEyNTU5YS05OGU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n1Fbmu66TSnoXeHfXmU4SnzwXIXv8w61D4B9S4QRVi4=)**

**House GOP's money machine** _"House Republicans in competitive districts dominated their Democratic counterparts in fundraising during the first three months of 2023, according to an Axios analysis."_ We won't sugarcoat it, team: Dark money-backed Republicans in the House are out-raising us and have a _major_ financial edge ahead of 2024.

We already knew 2024 was set to be one of the most competitive – and expensive – elections to date. But Kevin McCarthy and four other House Republicans announced that they raked in over **$50 million** for the first fiscal quarter of the year.

Look, National Republicans have made it clear they'll spare no expense trying to unseat for-the-people Democrats like Greg Landsman, Marcy Kaptur, and Emilia Sykes — _who have already been named some of the most vulnerable Democrats up for re-election in 2024._

**_Every single penny_ we raise will go toward helping us close the gap in our fundraising and ensure that Ohio Democrats have the resources needed to build winning campaigns and take back control of the House in 2024. **

**[With the help of this grassroots team, we can close this gap and end April off strong. Please chip in $5, or whatever you can, to help us reach our $14,000 end-of-month fundraising goal before Sunday's deadline »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62575804/403908297/-1720848229?refcode=em23_230430_eom3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230430_eom3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=05a2559a-98e7-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&amounts=5,10,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNWEyNTU5YS05OGU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n1Fbmu66TSnoXeHfXmU4SnzwXIXv8w61D4B9S4QRVi4=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [EXPRESS DONATE $5→](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62575805/403908298/-1720848229?refcode=em23_230430_eom3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230430_eom3_fndd_gq_act___ODP_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=05a2559a-98e7-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&express_lane=true&amount=5&amounts=5,10,25,50,100&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNWEyNTU5YS05OGU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n1Fbmu66TSnoXeHfXmU4SnzwXIXv8w61D4B9S4QRVi4=)

Peter,
_**You can still win an exciting trip to Tampa, Florida!
**_
The Countdown to the Majority Dinner Celebration is on May 18th, and I would love for you to join me. This event is a chance for us to come together and celebrate the momentum of House Republicans. And, as a special thank you for your support, I am offering you the opportunity to win an unforgettable trip to Tampa and attend the event as my guest.

clean_body: | | | | Scan the Code | Peter, You can still win an exciting trip to Tampa, Florida! The Countdown to the Majority Dinner Celebration is on May 18th, and I would love for you to join me. This event is a chance for us to come together and celebrate the momentum of House Republicans. And, as a special thank you for your support, I am offering you the opportunity to win an unforgettable trip to Tampa and attend the event as my guest. Not only will you have a chance to win this amazing trip, but your donation will also go towards supporting our efforts to take back the House in 2022. I cannot stress enough how important this election is, and I would be honored to have you join me at this event to celebrate our progress and our future. We will take care of your admission pass, flight, and hotel stay for you and a guest! Thank you for your continued support, and I hope to see you in Tampa! Sincerely, Lori Chavez-DeRemer Congresswoman OR-05 | | | | | | | | | Even as a Congresswoman, Lori’s most important title is mom. That’s how she knows the issues your family faces, and that’s why she fights everyday to make life better for every Oregon family, and every American. Every year, gun violence claims the lives of tens of thousands of Americans. We cannot afford to sit back and do nothing while our communities are devastated by these senseless acts of violence.

Stop Gun Violence PAC is committed to electing gun violence prevention champions at every level of government. Our goal is to pass life-saving gun legislation that will help to reduce the number of deaths and injuries caused by firearms.

But we can't do it alone. We need your support to fund our efforts and make a difference in the fight against gun violence.

clean_body: Wanted to make sure you didn't miss this. | Peter, We wanted to bump this email from Brian ahead of tonight’s midnight deadline. If we want to end our gun violence epidemic, there is no better way than to invest in candidates who will challenge the gun lobby. Stop Gun Violence PAC is committed to making that a reality. But we need your help.= | = = Peter, Brian here. Sorry to interrupt your Sunday, but we have an important deadline tonight. Stop Gun Violence PAC is investing in critical races across the country to elect gun violence prevention champions, pass lifesaving gun legislation, and save lives. But we need a concerted effort from supporters like you. = | = Every year, gun violence claims the lives of tens of thousands of Americans. We cannot afford to sit back and do nothing while our communities are devastated by these senseless acts of violence. Stop Gun Violence PAC is committed to electing gun violence prevention champions at every level of government. Our goal is to pass life-saving gun legislation that will help to reduce the number of deaths and injuries caused by firearms. But we can't do it alone. We need your support to fund our efforts and make a difference in the fight against gun violence. = | | | = | = | = | = | = Change starts with us. Brian Lemek | | = Stop Gun Violence PAC Stop Gun Violence PAC 600 PENNSYLVANIA AVE SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States | = If you do not wish to receive emails about this important movement to end gun violence, please

name: Ilhan Omar HQ
subject: quick note
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Hey team,
We know it's Sunday, and that we've been reaching out a lot lately. That's why we'll keep this quick.
In just a few short hours, we close the books on our April fundraising, and we're reaching out to some of our top supporters to help us cross the finish line strong.
Your support is one of the reasons our movement is as strong as ever, and why we're in a great position this early in the campaign cycle. But if you could add your first online contribution to Ilhan before tonight's deadline, it'd mean a lot. Here's the best link:
**[https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/April-30-deadline](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-apr23.QUZUGP&refcode=em230430-2-t2&t=1&akid=136%2E791427%2EkVauKf)**
Thank you so much,
Team Ilhan

PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can **[unsubscribe here](https://act.ilhanomar.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?source=em-footer&t=2&akid=136%2E791427%2EkVauKf)**. You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=3&akid=136%2E791427%2EkVauKf)**.
Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=4&akid=136%2E791427%2EkVauKf)
Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=136_791427_kVauKf&akid=136%2E791427%2EkVauKf)**.
Ilhan for Congress

Washington D.C., 20033

| Hi, it’s Jane Fonda. I know you heard from us a lot this week so I’ll keep this short.

Our April fundraising deadline is tonight at midnight and a generous supporter pledged to match every contribution up to $10,000 before our deadline.

Your support means so much to us, and I hope you take advantage of this opportunity to support the Jane Fonda Climate PAC. With each month, the urgency of our movement grows as the window to take action closes. We need all hands on deck to make progress and protect our planet.

name: Becca B.
subject: It’s hard not to take it personally
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for U
body:
Seeing you stand with me through the difficult moments makes all of my work worthwhile.
Peter,
It weighs on you.
Going toe-to-toe with Lauren Boebert and Marjorie Taylor Greene on the Oversight Committee.
Fielding hateful calls and comments directed at me and my staff.
Watching anti-LGBTQ+ bills pass through the halls of Congress.
I’ve always said that being a middle school teacher was some of the best prep work I could have done before choosing to run for Congress, and I still stand by it. It helped me build a thick skin, learn how to think quickly on my feet, and diffuse arguments among peers.
But some days, this work can be extremely difficult. It’s hard not to feel personally impacted when your humanity and the dignity of those that you love is being put up for debate. Today’s political environment is not for the faint of heart, but I’m honored to be a voice for reason and respect through it all.
Knowing that you’re still standing with me makes all of this work worthwhile, Peter. And ahead of tonight’s big deadline, it would mean the world to see a donation come in from you on ActBlue.
Here’s the link to give: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/BBVT-AL-Email-April2023-FR-EOM?refcode=deadline](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62576544/403911278/-665142517?refcode=E21342-20230430-Deadline-SL-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=27&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNDNkN2RjZS05YmU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IeKH01s1RUE5Z3yxSGUoZRUY8Im1zJrzvdltvlF67qg=)
A little reassurance that my grassroots supporters stand behind me while things intensify in Washington means a lot.
As always: Thank you, thank you, thank you.
– Becca B.

Becca for Vermont
P.O. Box 291
Burlington, VT 05302

name: VoteVets.org
subject: LAST CALL: Humbly asking you to chip in $5 before tonight’s deadline. Let us explain why.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
We’re hoping people who understand our impact and the importance of our goals will chip in tonight.
#
Derek -
If everyone receiving this email contributed $5 to VoteVets before our midnight deadline, we’d CRUSH our fundraising goals and have the resources we need to run our entire program in 2023.
$5 to help us finish the job and kick Hannity and Ingraham off of our Military bases.
$5 to help us continue our work saving and helping Veterans who are the target of far-right disinformation campaigns.
$5 to hold every Republican who voted to cut $30 billion from the Department of Veterans Affairs accountable and make sure they NEVER hold elected office again.
Not everyone who reads this email will give $5. That is why we’re hoping people who understand our impact and the importance of our goals will chip in tonight.
It all adds up. So if $5 is something you can afford tonight, please rest assured we will use it in a way you can be proud of.

Friend, I just want to start off by saying - I'm so sorry.

I hate that I have to drop into your inbox like this on the day of my End of Month Deadline, but I'm humbly asking you to **_read this all the way through._** If you get to the end and still think you can't help save the country from the Left's total control of our country, then I hope you enjoy Joe Biden controlling every aspect of your life.

This month I'm way off track of my goal. I can't stress this enough, but I'm trying to build the team that restores the American Dream. **_That means we have two options:_**

1\. Keep allowing the Left to abuse their power and go after anyone that believes in conservative values.

[**2\. | | | Peter, I’m disappointed.
---
**Our end-of-month deadline is TONIGHT at MIDNIGHT, and we’ve noticed you still haven’t pitched in to help us meet our goal.**

I know that Joe Biden’s economy has everyone pinching pennies, but we can’t start undoing his damage if we don’t hold onto our house majority.

No matter how much you can give, every contribution makes a difference.

I’ve seen on the inside the devastation that the Left is doing to our country, and I won’t stand by idly while they tear apart the America I put my life on the line for as a Green Beret.

_And I know you don’t want to sit on the sidelines either._

[**There are just HOURS left. clean_body: | | | Peter, I’m disappointed. Our end-of-month deadline is TONIGHT at MIDNIGHT, and we’ve noticed you still haven’t pitched in to help us meet our goal. I know that Joe Biden’s economy has everyone pinching pennies, but we can’t start undoing his damage if we don’t hold onto our house majority. No matter how much you can give, every contribution makes a difference. I’ve seen on the inside the devastation that the Left is doing to our country, and I won’t stand by idly while they tear apart the America I put my life on the line for as a Green Beret. And I know you don’t want to sit on the sidelines either. | | In Service, Col. Hello there 👋 — it’s Ashley.
**I have a personal request to make, but first, let’s talk about why this race matters.** While Kevin McCarthy and Brian Fitzpatrick are focused on fighting culture wars and stripping away our fundamental freedoms, working families in our district want to know how they will find good-paying jobs, pay their bills, and help their children get off to the best possible start in life.
This is what happens when we have corrupt politicians like Fitzpatrick in Congress with no skin in the game. It’s also why I’m determined to finish the job I started when I ran against him last cycle and fight for the meaningful policies Pennsylvanians deserve in Congress.
But it’s going to take all of us to defeat Fitzpatrick’s corporate donors. So I’m coming to you directly:

| **Two courts hand AG losses as transgender rule blocked** A St. Louis County circuit judge on April 26 issued a preliminary stay blocking Missouri Attorney General Andrew Bailey from restricting access to gender-affirming care in Missouri following a day of legal maneuvering that resulted in Bailey suffering setbacks in both state and federal court. The judge plans to issue a more definitive ruling on May 1.

Bailey, a Republican, filed an emergency regulation earlier this month seeking to unilaterally impose tight restrictions on the medical services available to transgender Missourians. Although the attorney general has no authority under state law to regulate medical care, Bailey is attempting to do so anyway under the auspices Missouri’s consumer protection laws.

The American Civil Liberties Union and Lambda Legal, an organization that defends LGBTQ rights, filed a lawsuit in state court on behalf of transgender patients and medical providers arguing Bailey’s action is an unconstitutional attempt by an executive branch official to usurp legislative authority and enact law under the guise of administrative regulation. The plaintiffs requested a temporary restraining order blocking the regulation, which had been scheduled to take effect April 27, until the case can be fully adjudicated.

In an apparent attempt to forestall a state court ruling, Bailey unexpectedly attempted to have the case moved to federal court shortly before a hearing on the temporary restraining order was scheduled to begin. However, during a brief hearing, U.S. District Court Judge Henry Autrey said federal courts have no jurisdiction over legal claims exclusively involving state law and denied Bailey’s request for transfer.

“You can’t just file something in federal court just because you want to be in federal court,” Autrey said, according the Missouri Independent. “There is something that sometimes bites people in the behind called jurisdiction.”

Bailey then attempted further delay by filing a motion for a change of judge in the state case. The motion was granted, but the scheduled hearing was pushed back by only a couple hours. Shortly after the hearing, St. Louis Circuit Court Judge Ellen Ribaudo, the replacement judge, stayed enforcement of Bailey’s regulation until she rules on the restraining order. Ribaudo also chided Bailey for failing to submit a brief outlining his position prior to the hearing.
---
|
---
|
---
Sen. Steven Roberts, D-St. Louis poses with St. Louis students in the Missouri Senate chambers as part of a legislative visit to the Missouri Capitol.
|
---
| **Senate approves $49.92 billion state operating budget**
The Senate on April 26 voted for a $49.92 billion state operating budget for the 2024 fiscal year that includes $4.4 billion more in spending authority than previously approved by the House of Representatives. The two chambers must now hammer out a final budget by a hard constitutional deadline of 6 p.m. on May 5.

However, those negotiations had yet to begin by the time lawmakers adjourned for the week. When they return to the Capitol on May 1, just a few days will remain for negotiators to work out the substantial differences between the two versions and bring the various appropriations bills that make up the budget to final votes in both chambers. If lawmakers miss the deadline, the budget process must start over in a special legislative session. The new fiscal year begins July 1.

A $2.8 billion plan to widen the entire length of Interstate 70 across Missouri that the Senate added to the operating budget accounts for much of the cost difference between the two plans. Although the House omitted I-70 funding from the operating budget, it did authorize a more limited $859 million plan to improve portions on the highway in a separate appropriations bill for capital improvement projects.

The Senate version also includes $461 million to increase pay for home care workers who provide services to elderly or disabled Missourians in an effort to reverse a worsening shortage of workers in the field. In addition, the Senate restored $78 million to expand preschool options throughout the state, funding requested by the governor but stricken by the House. Even with the extra spending, the Senate plan still leaves $2.5 billion from an unprecedented revenue surplus sitting untouched in the state treasury.

As happened in the House, Senate debate focused on whether to include language in the appropriations bills to prohibit “diversity, equity and inclusion” programs in Missouri. Such efforts are prominent in both government and the private sector as a means of fostering stronger ties with historically marginalized groups and attracting and retaining workers. Although the House inserted anti-DEI language in the bills, a Senate committee stripped it out. Hardline-conservatives attempted to restore the language on the Senate floor but, following hours of discussion, were unsuccessful.
---
|
---
| **Senate republicans vote to make it harder to amend state constitution**
The Senate on April 27 voted 24-10 along straight party lines for a proposed constitutional amendment intended to make it harder to amend the Missouri Constitution. The measure, which Republicans supported and Democrats opposed, returns to the House of Representatives, which approved a substantially different version of it in February.

As originally passed by the lower chamber, House Joint Resolution 43 sought to raise the threshold for voter ratification of constitutional amendments from the simple majority that has been required for two centuries to a 60 percent supermajority. The Senate version of HJR 43 would create a two-tiered system under which an amendment could be ratified by either a 57-percent statewide supermajority or by both a simple majority statewide and the approval of a majority of voters in at least five of Missouri’s eight congressional districts.

The Senate version also would raise the bar for the General Assembly to put proposed constitutional amendments on ballot, requiring a measure to first be approved by 57 percent majorities in each chamber instead of simple majorities. It also would prohibit the legislature from altering statutory proposals placed on the ballot via imitative petition within five years of passage unless 57 percent lawmakers in each chamber support doing so. Constitutional amendments proposed by the initiative would not be subject to the waiting period on legislative tinkering.

The House can either grant final passage to the Senate version or seek to negotiate a compromise version. If HJR 43 clears the legislature, it automatically would go on the November 2024 statewide ballot unless the governor exercises his constitutional authority to set an earlier election date. Ratification would require just a simple majority.

| **HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS **
The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19).

SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing.

Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and [download applications](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=4853c93f7c&e=a088917f89). Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email [disastercustomerservice@sba.gov](mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov) for more information on SBA disaster assistance.
---
|
---
| **SAFE SHELTERING RESOURCES** Sheltering in place can be difficult for anyone, and some of our neighbors might not be safe due to violence at home. If you or someone you know needs help, please visit [Safe Connections](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=77a6a5a009&e=a088917f89) or call _314-531-2003_.

You can also contact [ALIVE](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=ec967d2629&e=a088917f89) at _314-993-2777_.

| **RX OUTREACH EXPANDS ELIGIBILITY **
Below is a memo from a trusted non-profit:

As a non-profit, mail-order pharmacy, Rx Outreach is uniquely positioned to help stop the spread of COVID-19 for people who are quarantined and those who may be afraid to travel outside of their homes to the clinic or pharmacy.

Rx Outreach lifted its income guidelines during this crisis so that **anyone, regardless of income, can participate in their program.**

Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free.

---
|
---
| **LOW-COST INSULIN** Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. In the wake of COVID-19, it has become even more difficult for those who rely on insulin to afford the inflated cost. Eli Lilly and Company has introduced a program to purchase Basaglar insulin at significant cost savings. Please call Lilly Diabetes Solution Center at 833-808-1234 between 9 am and 9 pm, M-F to see if you qualify for these savings. More information can be found at the [Eli Lilly and Co. clean_body: This week in Jefferson City | | | | | | | Capitol Report April 30, 2023 | | | | Contact Senator Roberts 201 W. Capitol Ave., Rm 329 Jefferson City, MO 65101 (573) 751-4415 Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov | | | | | | | | | | | | Two courts hand AG losses as transgender rule blocked A St. Louis County circuit judge on April 26 issued a preliminary stay blocking Missouri Attorney General Andrew Bailey from restricting access to gender-affirming care in Missouri following a day of legal maneuvering that resulted in Bailey suffering setbacks in both state and federal court. The judge plans to issue a more definitive ruling on May 1. Bailey, a Republican, filed an emergency regulation earlier this month seeking to unilaterally impose tight restrictions on the medical services available to transgender Missourians. Although the attorney general has no authority under state law to regulate medical care, Bailey is attempting to do so anyway under the auspices Missouri’s consumer protection laws. The American Civil Liberties Union and Lambda Legal, an organization that defends LGBTQ rights, filed a lawsuit in state court on behalf of transgender patients and medical providers arguing Bailey’s action is an unconstitutional attempt by an executive branch official to usurp legislative authority and enact law under the guise of administrative regulation. The plaintiffs requested a temporary restraining order blocking the regulation, which had been scheduled to take effect April 27, until the case can be fully adjudicated. In an apparent attempt to forestall a state court ruling, Bailey unexpectedly attempted to have the case moved to federal court shortly before a hearing on the temporary restraining order was scheduled to begin. However, during a brief hearing, U.S. District Court Judge Henry Autrey said federal courts have no jurisdiction over legal claims exclusively involving state law and denied Bailey’s request for transfer. “You can’t just file something in federal court just because you want to be in federal court,” Autrey said, according the Missouri Independent. “There is something that sometimes bites people in the behind called jurisdiction.” Bailey then attempted further delay by filing a motion for a change of judge in the state case. The motion was granted, but the scheduled hearing was pushed back by only a couple hours. Shortly after the hearing, St. Louis Circuit Court Judge Ellen Ribaudo, the replacement judge, stayed enforcement of Bailey’s regulation until she rules on the restraining order. Ribaudo also chided Bailey for failing to submit a brief outlining his position prior to the hearing. | | Sen. Steven Roberts, D-St. Louis poses with St. Louis students in the Missouri Senate chambers as part of a legislative visit to the Missouri Capitol. | | Senate approves $49.92 billion state operating budget The Senate on April 26 voted for a $49.92 billion state operating budget for the 2024 fiscal year that includes $4.4 billion more in spending authority than previously approved by the House of Representatives. The two chambers must now hammer out a final budget by a hard constitutional deadline of 6 p.m. on May 5. However, those negotiations had yet to begin by the time lawmakers adjourned for the week. When they return to the Capitol on May 1, just a few days will remain for negotiators to work out the substantial differences between the two versions and bring the various appropriations bills that make up the budget to final votes in both chambers. If lawmakers miss the deadline, the budget process must start over in a special legislative session. The new fiscal year begins July 1. A $2.8 billion plan to widen the entire length of Interstate 70 across Missouri that the Senate added to the operating budget accounts for much of the cost difference between the two plans. Although the House omitted I-70 funding from the operating budget, it did authorize a more limited $859 million plan to improve portions on the highway in a separate appropriations bill for capital improvement projects. The Senate version also includes $461 million to increase pay for home care workers who provide services to elderly or disabled Missourians in an effort to reverse a worsening shortage of workers in the field. In addition, the Senate restored $78 million to expand preschool options throughout the state, funding requested by the governor but stricken by the House. Even with the extra spending, the Senate plan still leaves $2.5 billion from an unprecedented revenue surplus sitting untouched in the state treasury. As happened in the House, Senate debate focused on whether to include language in the appropriations bills to prohibit “diversity, equity and inclusion” programs in Missouri. Such efforts are prominent in both government and the private sector as a means of fostering stronger ties with historically marginalized groups and attracting and retaining workers. Although the House inserted anti-DEI language in the bills, a Senate committee stripped it out. Hardline-conservatives attempted to restore the language on the Senate floor but, following hours of discussion, were unsuccessful. | | Senate republicans vote to make it harder to amend state constitution The Senate on April 27 voted 24-10 along straight party lines for a proposed constitutional amendment intended to make it harder to amend the Missouri Constitution. The measure, which Republicans supported and Democrats opposed, returns to the House of Representatives, which approved a substantially different version of it in February. As originally passed by the lower chamber, House Joint Resolution 43 sought to raise the threshold for voter ratification of constitutional amendments from the simple majority that has been required for two centuries to a 60 percent supermajority. The Senate version of HJR 43 would create a two-tiered system under which an amendment could be ratified by either a 57-percent statewide supermajority or by both a simple majority statewide and the approval of a majority of voters in at least five of Missouri’s eight congressional districts. The Senate version also would raise the bar for the General Assembly to put proposed constitutional amendments on ballot, requiring a measure to first be approved by 57 percent majorities in each chamber instead of simple majorities. It also would prohibit the legislature from altering statutory proposals placed on the ballot via imitative petition within five years of passage unless 57 percent lawmakers in each chamber support doing so. Constitutional amendments proposed by the initiative would not be subject to the waiting period on legislative tinkering. The House can either grant final passage to the Senate version or seek to negotiate a compromise version. If HJR 43 clears the legislature, it automatically would go on the November 2024 statewide ballot unless the governor exercises his constitutional authority to set an earlier election date. Ratification would require just a simple majority. | | 2023 SENATE BILLS \- Creates new provisions relating to financial institutions \- Enacts new law relating to wage range inquiries by employees and prospective employees \- Modifies provisions relating to the offense of unlawful use of weapons \- Creates certificates of exemplary conduct and good moral character issued by circuit courts \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution \- Designates "James W Brooks Memorial Highway" in St. Louis City and St. Louis County \- Creates the offense of unlawful possession by a minor \- Modifies the practice of dentistry to include the prescription and administration of vaccines \- Specifies that the St. Louis City school board shall fill any vacancy that occurs in such school board outside of the normal election cycle \- Authorizes counties to freeze the real property assessed values of certain senior citizens | | "Signed By Governor" | "Truly Agreed Bills" | | | | | HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19). SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing. Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and . Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov for more information on SBA disaster assistance. | | SAFE SHELTERING RESOURCES Sheltering in place can be difficult for anyone, and some of our neighbors might not be safe due to violence at home. **GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

Friends, I hate to bother you on a Sunday, but I just wanted to reach out one more time before tonight’s end-of-month deadline. Asking folks like you to chip in any of your hard-earned money is my least favorite part of campaigning, trust me.
| Friends, I hate to bother you on a Sunday, but I just wanted to reach out one more time before tonight’s end-of-month deadline.

Asking folks like you to chip in any of your hard-earned money is my least favorite part of campaigning, trust me.

But with Republicans preparing to spend millions to defeat me and take back OH-01, we can’t afford to fall short tonight.

So please, can I count on you to make a donation of any size before midnight? name: Brandon Presley
subject: Are you in or out?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brandon Presley Campaign
body:
This is for all the marbles. | Time is running out, Peter!

We've got our first major public filing deadline in just a few hours and we're still short $2,633 from hitting our fundraising goal.

This is a crucial moment where I need your support the most. Click here to donate: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/presley_bax_em_2023_eom?refcode=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62577354/403914590/960250762?refcode=ea.8ecaba5f-a2e7-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ms=ea.8ecaba5f-a2e7-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGVjYWJhNWYtYTJlNy1lZDExLThlOGItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=2hBrSIO1pkJ9vyPkeegrQqMFqzQpqb2DcxeIxR2Tqqw=).

Without you, our campaign risks falling behind and losing ground to Tate Reeves and his country club cronies. Let's show them that we won't back down or be intimidated.

Can I count on you to help us reach our fundraising goal and send a clear message to Reeves?

My mission is to give power back to the people of Mississippi, but we can only do that if we hit every fundraising goal, especially this one.

Every dollar counts, so please don't let this opportunity slip away, Peter. Click here to donate before it's too late: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/presley_bax_em_2023_eom?refcode=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62577355/403914591/960250762?refcode=ea.8ecaba5f-a2e7-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ms=ea.8ecaba5f-a2e7-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGVjYWJhNWYtYTJlNy1lZDExLThlOGItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=2hBrSIO1pkJ9vyPkeegrQqMFqzQpqb2DcxeIxR2Tqqw=).

I can’t thank you enough.

\- Brandon Presley

subject: One last message for you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#]Hi, it's Josh. I wanted to reach out personally and remind you that our fundraising deadline is tonight, and I need your support now more than ever.

In just a few hours, we’re facing our biggest deadline since an extremist judge in Texas banned access to a safe and effective abortion pill used by millions of women.

In 2016, I ran for Congress against an anti-choice extremist with the promise that I would fight to defend the right to choose. As your Congressman, I have fought to ensure that every individual can make their own decisions about their health, and I won’t stop now.

So if you can, will you chip in anything you can spare to help us hit our $10,000 goal:

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jg-fr-2304-eom?amount= [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jg-fr-2304-eom?amount=]

Thank you,

Josh
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. name: OcasioCortez.com
subject: quick update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
Here’s where we’re at.
#
Today is the **last** day of our end-of-month push to raise $400,000 to make sure Alexandria can remain in Congress and continue fighting for our progressive values.
We’re so close to reaching our goal.
[](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230430-2.nrXbWm&refcode=em20230430-4248-P_FND_EOM_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG)
Thanks to hundreds of gifts averaging just $16.17, we know that we'll be able to get over the finish line. But we need your help.
[Can we count on you to show up in these last few hours and support our movement with a contribution of $3 or more, so that we can close the gap on our goal before midnight?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230430-2.nrXbWm&refcode=em20230430-4248-P_FND_EOM_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230430-2.nrXbWm&refcode=em20230430-4248-P_FND_EOM_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG)**
We are the #1 small-dollar campaign in the House with the highest percentage of our resources coming from small donors. So these online contributions really matter to our campaign. When we set these goals, they’re not made-up — that’s why we’re asking for your help today.
Whether you can afford to make a donation today or not — we’re so grateful that you are a part of this collective movement for racial, social, economic, and environmental justice.
Pa’lante,
Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230430-2?refcode=em20230430-4248-P_FND_EOM_GFM&t=22&refcode2=4248_2526796_-r-3DG&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230430-4248-P_FND_EOM_GFM&t=23&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=24&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=25&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=26&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=27&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=4248%2E2526796%2E-r-3DG)

Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress

Friend, in 2022, with the odds stacked against us, Democrats made history by holding down and expanding our Senate majority. **After the conservative Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade in June, and Republicans started threatening a national abortion ban, we knew we couldn't rest for a second.**

Now, the GOP has taken back the House, and they're using their power at every level of government to attack our fundamental rights. Earlier this month, a Trump-appointed federal judge in Texas overturned FDA approval of mifepristone, the most commonly used method of abortion in America.

And now, Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has signed a bill to ban abortion after just six weeks – a point at which most women don't even know they are pregnant.

We need to get to work today if we want to have a chance at protecting our reproductive rights and holding Republicans accountable for their blatant attacks on our fundamental freedoms.

Our membership numbers will help determine whether or not we're able to keep control of the Senate and the White House and flip the House to stop a national abortion ban. I need you to stand with me and American Bridge to defeat the Republicans who are on a warpath to ban abortion nationwide.
**[Please, friend, help me reach our goal of activating 1,000 new April Democratic Memberships by midnight. Rush $25 right now. After the conservative Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade in June, and Republicans started threatening a national abortion ban, we knew we couldn't rest for a second. Now, the GOP has taken back the House, and they're using their power at every level of government to attack our fundamental rights. Earlier this month, a Trump-appointed federal judge in Texas overturned FDA approval of mifepristone, the most commonly used method of abortion in America. And now, Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has signed a bill to ban abortion after just six weeks – a point at which most women don't even know they are pregnant. We need to get to work today if we want to have a chance at protecting our reproductive rights and holding Republicans accountable for their blatant attacks on our fundamental freedoms. Our membership numbers will help determine whether or not we're able to keep control of the Senate and the White House and flip the House to stop a national abortion ban. I need you to stand with me and American Bridge to defeat the Republicans who are on a warpath to ban abortion nationwide. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = Thank you. – Cecile Richards | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. | | Paid for by AB PAC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. American Bridge 21st Century 800 Maine Ave SW Suite 400 Washington, DC 20024 United States

name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: On May 16th, equality for all, women controlling their bodies, voting rights, gun safety and more could be imperiled in Pennsylvania if the state house changes hands.
body:
special election
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta85b2c6b-0e5b-41b5-a419-791c98210261/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, [**Equality for all, women controlling their bodies, voting rights, gun safety and more could be imperiled in a few weeks in Pennsylvania.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1b4ed43d-e7d3-4d6e-8668-c628b11c92f4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Democrats won a majority in the PA House of Representatives this past election, part of a blue tsunami that crashed through The Keystone State in the midterms!_**_It gave us this incredible image of the first woman to become Speaker in PA's 200+ year history._** [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7a640046-4f85-43ee-b32c-c61c91c02018/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **_But in mere few weeks there are 2 special elections for open seats. With only a one-seat margin, if GOPers win both, they take control of the PA House of Representatives. __It would be a disaster, removing our Speaker, shutting down all progressive Governor Josh Shapiro wants to do and harming the 13 million Americans living there. And the_** _ _**_tens of millions who live in surrounding states impacted by how PA regulates guns, pollution of air and water and more. What happens in PA doesn't stay there--air & bullets don't see state lines._** _This is why we must support Heather Boyd, whose campaign centerpiece is "protecting women's bodily autonomy" and Trevor S. Finn, who promises to put the lives of children ahead of allowing criminals to conceal carry guns._ **_We can't let Republicans win back the chamber._** [**_Will you join us in giving $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to Democrats Heather Boyd & Trevor S. Finn to protect 13 million Pennsylvanians and tens of millions of additional Americans from being forced into a hell of MAGA Republicanism?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8a273676-b8a5-406e-a551-4545b961c84f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **Thank you for always fighting the fights that matter!** Cliff

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3f3e660b-28bb-43eb-a973-3c1961808314/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

body:
We need to keep it up, Peter | | |
#

| Joe, the numbers for April are not where we need them and that's going to set us back on our plans for endorsements. Nat
How short are we? Joe |
| **As of right now ~$2300 short of our online goal. I think we can close the gap since we still have a few hours until midnight.** NAT
We can definitely get there, I know exactly who to reach out to. JOE |
| Omg another email? 😂 NAT
No, it could be another idea. I’ve got a ton of ideas! JOE |

Alright, it’s another email... JOE |

Cannot, will not JOE |

body:
I’m so sorry to email you on a Sunday.

| Hi,

I’m so sorry to email you on a Sunday, but our April fundraising deadline is in just a few hours and we’re so close to reaching our ambitious goal, so I’m asking you to make a contribution right away.

Here’s a secure link to give now: [_**hillaryscholten.com/dpwillis67@gmail.com**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62578056/403916687/-43077042?refcode=BLU-HSC-20230430-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDZjZjNiYi1hNWU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2esDhgKEptuRqcHb61xlJa7Q6G_Q8L5RbfrvyjWbYx8=)

I hear that loud and clear – but the truth is that fundraising at this point in the election cycle determines the kinds of long-term investments we’re able to make in crucial resources like campaign staff and voter outreach.

Our team is organized and ready to win in 2024. Will you make sure we have a strong foundation with a contribution before tonight’s fundraising deadline?

Here’s that link one more time: [_**hillaryscholten.com/dpwillis67@gmail.com**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62578058/403916688/-43077042?refcode=BLU-HSC-20230430-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDZjZjNiYi1hNWU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2esDhgKEptuRqcHb61xlJa7Q6G_Q8L5RbfrvyjWbYx8=)

With so much appreciation,
Hillary

body:
I had to check in one more time before tonight’s April fundraising deadline.

| Hi Peter,

I know you’ve heard from me and my team a lot this week, but I had to check in one more time before tonight’s April fundraising deadline. With just a few hours before the end of the month, we’re still $1,823 short of our must-hit $15,000 goal.

If you are able, can I count on you to chip in at this personal link before midnight? [_**https://wildforcongress.com/q1-2023-dpwillis67@gmail.com**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62578043/403916675/428766023?refcode=BLU-SWC-20230430-2-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NTZjZjNiYi1hNWU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VPqo1lDeBPVbvuDHbeM-tbcBsqn8lTCx_M6j4A6T7Tc=)

Believe me, Peter, I hate asking for your hard-earned money — and I know I’ve been asking a lot. But Republicans are already running attack ads against me in their efforts to unseat me and hold the House majority, so we can’t afford to fall behind on a single goal.

Even a small contribution, like $10, can make a big difference as I work to defend this battleground seat. Can I count on you to chip in before midnight?

You can use this personal link: [_**https://wildforcongress.com/q1-2023-dpwillis67@gmail.com**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62578045/403916676/428766023?refcode=BLU-SWC-20230430-2-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NTZjZjNiYi1hNWU3LWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VPqo1lDeBPVbvuDHbeM-tbcBsqn8lTCx_M6j4A6T7Tc=)

Thank you,
Susan

name: Cori Bush
subject: Why I say 'your Congresswoman loves you'
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress
body:
As part of that love, I promise to always be transparent with you about where I'm at.
#
Peter,
I often sign with my emails with 'Your Congresswoman loves you'. **When I tell you that, it's not just words — it's something I genuinely mean.** I do this work because I love my district, my people, and you.
To me, radical love means radical honesty. It means being transparent with you about where I'm at. And it means asking for help when you need it.
So Peter, today in the spirit of that transparency, I come directly to you with an important question. **With just hours left before our final end-of-month fundraising deadline, I could use your help.[If you're able to, can you donate $5 to get us to our goal?](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230430-2.M4HCmi&refcode=em20230430-2608&t=1&akid=2608%2E1082990%2Efvq2jZ)**
**[Donate](https://act.coribush.org/go/2532?c0=cori-em-20230430-2.az55yY&refcode=em20230430-2608&t=20&akid=2608%2E1082990%2Efvq2jZ)**
To me, radical love is a powerful thing. It's what got me in this work in the first place, and keeps me in it even on the hardest days.
To me, it doesn't matter who are you — whether you love me or you hate me. I care if you ate today. I care if you have a roof over your head today. I care that you're safe today. **I care that you know you're loved today.**
**[I am determined to keep doing this work for as long as I can — for the love of our communities, and until we can get Justice for All. But I need your help to stay in the fight. Will you please chip in $5 — or whatever you can — to power our grassroots movement for bold, progressive change?](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230430-2.M4HCmi&refcode=em20230430-2608&t=21&akid=2608%2E1082990%2Efvq2jZ)**
Thank you for all of your support, Peter. Your Congressoman loves you,
Cori

**[Contribute ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cori-em-20230430-2?refcode=em20230430-2608-footer&t=22&refcode2=2608_1082990_fvq2jZ&akid=2608%2E1082990%2Efvq2jZ) **
---
Follow us on **[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/VoteCoriBush/)** , **[Twitter](https://twitter.com/CoriBush)** , **[Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/coribush/)** , and **[visit our website](https://coribush.org/)**. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Mail us: Cori Bush for Congress, 75 North Oaks Plaza, St. Louis, MO 63121.
Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress

As one of the Republicans’ top priority districts to flip in 2024, we can’t afford to fall short tonight or take our foot off the gas for one second. That’s how competitive this race is going to be.

So please, will you chip in to help us close the gap before midnight? A donation of any size will make a massive difference. We can’t afford to fall short here. Joe Biden won Georgia by 0.23% in 2020, and Georgia could be even closer in 2024. Every penny that grassroots supporters like you chip in will go directly to organizing efforts that could be the difference between victory and defeat in 2024.

**This is the last time we are reaching out before our critical deadline, so we are humbly asking:**

disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THEIR FUTURE
body:
| | | If you'd like to unsubscribe,[click here.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnl7NBjmTZL5F5oqdT0MFW4GLNp5FjRArZ2gECnWf63UZWzcCuYR-Es9PjYmAXR4JxZbuH_t8O228MjZNOxN7Lm2WoL5eKG3lZx13kI8WltFNG_zObdyET8MHkQjmDKw1IOR94ccwuLyhPEdZWBYz9177zcwucoJv5EXHzXHNMYCMEJNqqANsG7k7WC8jiEgM8zxjVZeGTnvwjm8pA_yhzSWJsyb7lDZYRkd9RFUz5L8v2CQzPASXG8kwn5rQQnifDkn5fSlarWWZTZplvKrlWCi6wsxaKo2b0r-GIKyEynhQjqMVfQxHqLnHZ7xbV4mHOHkJdW2tWNzU9vQ8AD_-5b0EiOdR0ElLGdDf4IUe0JFgfjwtd1CKzMWUgT7fo5G2_RVpz8ZkeQKmQpzChBjslhL/3vs/2d-sqGAPQMebd1KbTjMAwA/h0/_HB3jSz3iq3WX4WXHXt1j8UmOMPEjxi-Yb7lLlQsHRA) | |
---
Friend,

After my congressional race last year, I knew I couldn’t go back to the hospital and watch kids needlessly suffer because of bad policy choices being made by our representatives.

Research shows that 1 in 25 kindergarteners are not going to make it to age 40. Gun violence, the climate crisis, pandemics, and more... kids today have too many challenges, and too often their leaders aren’t listening to them.

**That’s where Their Future. Our Vote. comes in. Instead of playing politics with our kids, it's time to get political for our kids.**

**[I need you in this with me. Our first budget deadline since our launch is in just a few hours. Will you chip in $10 by midnight to help us raise the last $4,800 we need to fully fund our advocacy efforts?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yydMUN1nt5thY_dWpLP_JnBHwftUjZqeBp1GluIQkkSZZ9HWVCpdlZWYL--kVJs5E5Y35ryhyV-_LAHs-YV1m-b2jmmtZWtBvQhF8PrjavxVHVAxXDZOgHpvnwTvPFv90kFOdIeYatHiS5zdxbCh5lmg_ke036ljlxQlDR9TPHYLntCojn5Ion3ky90TPLixpssqJIiMJyQoVVDLuUm-lO-yid3d8Zvi45qyOKwKfM-H-oKIZic5B0eTHUlwL7DXbjFmPOk9gQI15VD1pHuMxImAu7ey-lnHUa8dHLMKxgTnZKggSWpFwdenoU5MlkBve98X4Lt7QlTPubjD4Mh0NoVwWWBwyIgMhZeA0zSxEjYQ/3vs/2d-sqGAPQMebd1KbTjMAwA/h1/nwhi0j7S2BvWgifDLZuTLTDXr9VVHZ8Mic2ZwzzMjJw)**

body:
Morning Consult has reported that Andy is the fifth most popular governor in the United States.
## MORNING CONSULT: 🚨"Poll: Beshear ranks fifth most popular governor in U.S. ahead of reelection bid"🚨
Great news, Peter — a new poll found that Andy remains one of the most popular governor's in the entire nation! And, _personally_ , I'm not surprised one bit – just take a look at his track record.

Since coming to office, Andy has achieved so much for the Commonwealth: creating jobs, promoting economic growth, and investing in vital infrastructure and healthcare. And all the while he's brought together bipartisan coalitions and facilitated Kentuckians working together for a brighter future. His accomplishments are why 63% of Kentucky voters approve of his time in office.

**But here's the reality check:** Andy won by just over _5,000 votes_ in 2019 — a tiny margin. Now, as the primary election quickly approaches, Andy will soon be competing with the full weight of the Republican Party supporting whichever candidate comes out on top May 16th. We're talking _**millions and millions of dollars**_ in attack ads from outside groups and interests who want to erase the progress Andy's made.

body:
| George Santos’ list of lies is growing EVERY DAY – so when we reached out to top Democrats to sign our letter demanding his resignation, we thought signatures would POUR IN. **__But we couldn’t have been more wrong.__** We’re sending our letter to Santos tomorrow morning at 9am sharp, and we need a SURGE of signatures before we do!

_If we don’t, Santos will think nobody cares about his lies and he’ll get away scot-free!_ [We’re asking one last time: Chip in $3 to add your name to our letter demanding George Santos resigns from Congress »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/44186?t=6&akid=7413%2E878179%2EpLWLwq) | [**DONATE $3 AND ADD YOUR NAME TO OUR LETTER »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/44186?t=7&akid=7413%2E878179%2EpLWLwq)
